_human_review.notes: US Stage B generated/autolabeled labels were reconciled by Codex for internal benchmark cleanup; project-owner approval is still required before promotion-ready status.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: US Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: US Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: For GLBA nonpublic personal information and HIPAA protected health information, we collect and process only for account servicing, care coordination, and fraud monitoring; we do not “sell” personal information as that term is used in California statutes.
Identity verification requires two matching data points and a one-time code; do not transmit images of IDs—upload via the secure portal with redaction guidance (e.g., show last 2 digits only).

Positive MNPI note: preliminary Q3 payer reimbursement rate changes and internal net charge‑off trend slides remain non‑public and may be material to PrairieBridge; do not discuss with analysts or investors before the scheduled earnings call is publicly webcast.

False‑positive bait control: consumer submissions containing strings like 123‑45‑6789, “CUSIP: NOT‑A‑CUSIP,” or “MRN: XX‑0000” are treated as examples and redacted; inclusion of the words “insider,” “leak,” or “earnings” does not convert a DSAR into a securities inquiry.
Disclosures to service providers include minimum necessary data; we require audit rights and breach notices, and we document all onward transfers in our internal registry.

Questions may be directed to compliance@prairiebridge.example; we also maintain a US toll‑free helpline listed on our public website, which provides general guidance but does not accept sensitive identifiers.
do not discuss with analysts or investors
we prohibit selective disclosure to market professionals
intentional disclosures are made via broad distribution or a Form 8‑K concurrently
non-intentional disclosures are cured promptly (no later than 24 hours or before the next trading day on our listing exchange)
we never request full SSN or account/routing numbers by email
preliminary Q3 payer reimbursement rate changes

must_detect.matched_text: For GLBA nonpublic personal information and HIPAA protected health information, we collect and process only for account servicing, care coordination, and fraud monitoring; we do not “sell” personal information as that term is used in California statutes.
Identity verification requires two matching data points and a one-time code; do not transmit images of IDs—upload via the secure portal with redaction guidance (e.g., show last 2 digits only).

Positive MNPI note: preliminary Q3 payer reimbursement rate changes and internal net charge‑off trend slides remain non‑public and may be material to PrairieBridge; do not discuss with analysts or investors before the scheduled earnings call is publicly webcast.

False‑positive bait control: consumer submissions containing strings like 123‑45‑6789, “CUSIP: NOT‑A‑CUSIP,” or “MRN: XX‑0000” are treated as examples and redacted; inclusion of the words “insider,” “leak,” or “earnings” does not convert a DSAR into a securities inquiry.

uncertain.matched_text: Retention: GLBA records are retained 7 years, HIPAA-designated records 6 years from creation, and marketing consents until revocation or 24 months of inactivity, whichever is earlier
uncertain.reason: Retention markers are relevant privacy controls but no explicit retention rule is listed in allowed rules.
PII if populated; here only the format is described and no specific rule exists for such internal IDs.
Would be PII if an actual value were present; here only a format is described and not clearly mapped to a listed rule.
ZIP code can be a quasi-identifier but is not covered by a specific allowed rule unless part of a combination.
